title: rubus tomentosus subsp. raddeanus
intro: Rubus tomentosus subsp. raddeanus, also known as Radde's raspberry or wild raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus tomentosus plant, a type of bramble that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus tomentosus subsp. raddeanus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
